Grace Ling · Runway
Editorial
4PM · La Jolla

The environment

The space is the
first service
delivered.

The embossed ceiling. The red chandelier. The checkerboard floor and the portrait on the wall. None of it is décor. All of it is intention — the physical expression of a brand that understands atmosphere as product, not backdrop.

Paul
Chief Creative Operations Officer
Jaylin
Chief Creative Officer

The origin

Forty years
in the making.
This is what's left.

Paul began his career at fifteen. That was 1985. Four decades of craft, culture, and relentless editing — built into a legacy salon in Canada that defined a community for over thirty years, until fire took it in 2023. What survived was everything that couldn't burn: the instinct, the standard, the way of seeing.

Jaylin came up at José Eber Salon in Beverly Hills before bringing that caliber to La Jolla — opening Jax & King in 2013 and building the brand's identity from the ground up. He is the dream. Paul is what makes the dream hold. In November 2024 they moved to this location and built 4PM from everything that came before.

Forty years.
One fire.
One address.

The doctrine

Forty years ago,
a fifteen-year-old picked up scissors.

He hasn't put them down.

We don't explain the work before we do it. We don't justify the space before you enter it. The quality speaks — or it doesn't. We believe it does.

01
Intentionality
No accidental moments. Every element — light, language, timing — is a deliberate choice.
02
Restraint
More is not better. Better is better. The edit is where craft lives.
03
Perception
We train for how it feels, not just how it looks. Guest experience is a system, not a mood.
04
Continuity
The brand doesn't turn off between appointments. The culture is the constant.
